ModMetaData.name: Mod Fixes Pack
ModMetaData.description:
A consolidated patch pack for large RimWorld mods. Each fix only activates if its target mod is loaded — safe to install even if you don't have all the patched mods. More fixes will be added over time.
CURRENT FIXES:
--- Dynamic Diplomacy (Continued) ---
- Terrain validation: rejects battle tiles and conquest targets on problematic terrain. Retries with better tiles before giving up. Works alongside Geological Landforms. Rejects: - Impassable mountains (vanilla mountain tiles) - Extreme cliffs from Geological Landforms - Cave topology - Ocean/water-covered tiles - Extreme temperature biomes (below -20°C or above 50°C) — covers Alpha Biomes, HMC Biomes, Vile's Biomes, etc. where pawns can't survive long enough to resolve a battle
- Shambler/infection deadlock: when pawns switch factions mid-battle (e.g., get infected and become shamblers), they're now cleaned from the battle faction lists so DD's win check works correctly. Prevents battle maps from staying open forever.
- Hard timeout failsafe: if a battle runs past 7 game days without ending, force it to end via DD's timeout mechanism.
- HSK-tuned event rates: reduces DD event frequency from vanilla pace (3-5 conquests per 60 days) to HSK pace (1-2 conquests per 90 days). Matches the slower HSK progression curve. Per-event changes: - NPC_Conquest: 3-5 per 60d with 0.5d spacing → 1-2 per 90d with 20d spacing - NPC_DiploChange: 2-4 per 60d with 5d spacing → 1-2 per 60d with 15d spacing - NPC_Expansion: 1-2 per 60d with 15d spacing → 1 per 90d with 30d spacing - NPC_Convert: 1-2 per 120d with 30d spacing → 1 per 180d with 60d spacing
--- Geological Landforms ---
- Shared terrain utility used by DD fixes. Checks tile topology (CliffAllSides, CliffValley, etc.) to avoid problematic battle maps.
--- Real Ruins ---
NEW MOD FIX: Real Ruins - World-tile relocation: POI icons moved off Mountainous/Impassable tiles via 10-ring BFS search for a valid neighbor. No more Factory/Military Base/Camp icons stuck in mountains. - Local-map Scatter relocation: ruin blueprints that would overlap thick-roof rock / natural stone are relocated to cleaner cells on the map (30-candidate search). Covers all 13 POI types, background scatter, medium/large/POI gen steps. - Effect: every Real Ruins icon on your world map is reachable from the map edge without having to mine through a mountain.
HSK-style tuning (auto-applied when both Real Ruins AND HSK are loaded — more hostiles, harder to get stuff): - hostileChance: 10% → 35% (ruins are actual threats, not free loot stops) - trapChance: 0.1% → 0.4% (4x more traps) - forceMultiplier: 1.0 → 1.5 (hostile forces 50% stronger) - scavengingMultiplier: 1.0 → 1.75 (most valuable items stripped before you arrive) - itemCostLimit: 1000 → 400 silver (no premium loot) - ruinsCostCap: 1 billion → 300k silver (no jackpot mega-bases) - abandonedLocations: 20% → 50% (more truly abandoned POIs) - deteriorationMultiplier: left at 0.0 — integrity falloff already damages edges naturally; double-penalty would make leftovers useless
Tuning only activates if HSK is detected. Non-HSK modpacks keep Real Ruins vanilla defaults.
--- MultiLang Colorful Traits ---
NEW MOD FIX: MultiLang Colorful Traits auto-extend - MultiLang Colorful Traits colors traits by QUALITY (Unique / Good / Neutral / Bad / Terrible) using hand-curated XML lists for Core, HSK, RJW, AlienRaces, and CE. Any trait not on those lists stays uncolored — and its own "Dump" workflow requires manual XML editing per mod. - This fix runs after MultiLang, inspects every TraitDef in the modlist, skips degrees MultiLang already colored (manual classification always wins), and auto-classifies the rest into MultiLang's 5 tiers using an identical color palette so everything looks visually consistent. - Classification is mechanical: it scores each degree from skillGains, statOffsets/Factors, the parent TraitDef's disabledWorkTypes/Tags, random/forced mental states, hunger rate, pain modifiers, and required work tags. - 8-tier classification using all the colors defined in MultiLang's ColorBase.xml: * Legendary (score >= +7): pure bright green #00FF00 — exceptional positive * Good (+3 to +6): muted green #9AB973 — solid positive * Neutral (-2 to +2): plain white (label left untouched — reduces visual noise so only meaningful traits stand out) * Bad (-3 to -4): salmon orange #FF9966 — noticeable downside * Terrible (-5 to -6): red #ED2939 — serious downside * Nightmare (<= -7): pure red #FF0000 — worst of the worst * Unique (strongly flavored mechanic, e.g. large psychic sensitivity swing): blue #0000FF * Restrictive (disables work types/tags, or has required work tags): purple #9370DB — shape-defining trait regardless of score - Restrictive and Unique take priority over the score-based tiers so build-shaping and quirk mechanics are always visible at a glance. - Log example: [Mod Fixes Pack] MultiLang: auto-classified traits (Good:60, Neutral:55, Bad:40, Terrible:20, Unique:10, skipped 185 already-colored).
Net effect: before the fix, ~30% of HSK modpack traits are colored (MultiLang's hard-coded lists). After, ~100% of traits are colored. MultiLang's hand-tuning is preserved on traits it knows; we just fill the gaps.
Auto-disables if MultiLang Colorful Traits isn't loaded.
COMPATIBILITY: - All fixes are conditional — inactive if the target mod isn't loaded - Zero overhead if a mod is missing - Safe to install on any save - Works with HSK (Hardcore SK) and vanilla
All logs are prefixed with [Mod Fixes Pack] for easy filtering in the debug console.
